title: Tyber Zann
content:

{{Quote|I am Tyber Zann. Corruption is my weapon. And while the galaxy is distracted by civil war, I will strike.|Tyber Zann|<ref name="FOC" />}}
'''Tyber Zann''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] from [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]] who rose to prominence after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and led his own crime syndicate, known as the [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]], a powerful underworld [[consortium]]. He would wage a campaign against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as revenge for expelling him from the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], while sabotaging the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] as well.

At the height of the Galactic Civil War, the crime lord Zann rose as one of the underworld's masterminds, to the chagrin of more powerful kingpins like the [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] and [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|Everyone has a price.|Tyber Zann|<ref name="FOC" />}}
[[File:Tzann.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Tyber Zann, rising crime lord.]]

Tyber Zann was born on [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]] in [[34 BBY/Legends|34 BBY]], and first worked in the family firm [[Zann Remanufacturing]], which specialized in refitting outdated [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] and [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] hardware for civilian use. He became bored with the linear business, and started running illicit [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] games and book-making, which he found to provide the excitement and revenue that he sought.<ref name="Underworld News" />

In order to arm his gambling ring's enforcers, Zann attempted to steal an arms shipment from one of the company's warehouses. The security forces caught him and reported the attempted theft to his father, who as punishment, arranged for him to be enrolled as a [[Cadet/Legends|cadet]] in the Imperial [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]].<ref name="Underworld News" />

===Rise of the Consortium===
====Imperial service====
{{Quote|I was rapidly working my way up through the Imperial academy. But some took exception to my creative solutions for advancement and had me expelled. I vowed to make them pay for this indignity.|Tyber Zann|<ref name="FOC" />}}
Operating out of the Academy, Zann established a new [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] ring - the Zann Consortium. The facility's lax security made it simple for him to steal Imperial weaponry and smuggle it off-world, to his partner, the [[Talortai/Legends|Talortai]] [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] [[Urai Fen]], who sold them on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]]. A top performer, Zann used the credentials this granted him to get on training missions where he could form a network of contacts for the smuggling trade. The operation became one of the largest in the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], and he started to evoke the attention of more powerful criminals, namely the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]. Jabba coordinated a plan to catch Zann during a smuggling run shortly before his graduation. Fortunately for Tyber, Urai intercepted transmissions between Imperial authorities about the plot. Although he was able to avoid any prosecution, Zann was forced to leave the Academy in contempt.<ref name="Underworld News" />

====Alliance with Jabba====
{{Quote|Time is money.|Tyber Zann|<ref name="FOC" />}}
[[File:Jabba_SWSB.png|thumb|right|170px|Jabba Desilijic Tiure was Zann's chief competitor.]]
Despite his misgivings, Zann perceived that to continue building the Zann Consortium he would need to concede to an uneasy pact with Jabba. Subsequently, he began running smuggling operations for his new ally in exchange for vehicles and vessels, while also concealing assets from his fellow crime lord.<ref name="Underworld News" /> Zann never intended this arrangement with Jabba to be permanent, and after a year he had gained word that the Hutt kingpin had spent nearly half his wealth on a unique [[Carbonite Sith Army holocron|artifact]], though Tyber did not know anything of its origins.<ref name="FOC" />

====Raid over Mandalore====
The Zann Consortium was still small, and Zann intended to establish communications with a black market on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], though first he needed to show them an example of his capabilities, and develop a reputation that the Consortium was a force-to-be-reckoned with. After being informed by one of his [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] that the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Supreme Strategist]] was traveling to the [[Hutt asteroid station|asteroid station]] above [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], Zann seized the opportunity. Traveling to Mandalore, the Consortium flotilla destroyed the [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|V-wing]] escort sent to prepare for the strategist's arrival. Shortly afterwards, the strategist arrived in his [[Keldabe-class cruiser]], though the Consortium ambushed him. During the battle, the strategist's engines were destroyed, his ship was boarded, and he was taken prisoner. This outcome enabled for the Consortium to have a long-term source of ships produced by the [[Mandal Hypernautics Corporation]]. Zann assigned one of his [[defiler]]s to establish communications with the black market on Nal Hutta, and Urai was sent to buy [[Disruptor/Legends|disruptor]] technology for the Consortium's [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]].<ref name="FOC" />

====Theft of the artifact====
Zann's agents decoded a message from an Imperial outpost on [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]] that authorized Jabba to land on the planet's surface. Recognizing that the Hutt intended to sell the artifact to the Empire, Zann lead a taskforce along with Urai and several squads of mercenaries to steal the artifact before the transaction could be made. Posing as [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]] forces, the Imperial [[Fleet Commander/Legends|fleet commander]] was convinced, though hesitantly, that the group were arriving separately from the main group to safeguard the artifact. The taskforce, aboard a [[TZ-86 transport]] located a clearing in the dense foliage and landed there. Ordering [[Alpha Squad (Zann)|Alpha Squad]] to scout ahead and make sure that their path to the Hutt landing site was clear, Zann watched as they perished to [[Felucian spore plant]]s. Fortunately for the remainder, they had brought a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[F9-TZ Transport]], which Zann ordered his troops to unload, and to deploy a [[Mobile Defense Unit]] with a [[rocket pod]], lest somebody stumble across the landing site. The group proceeded to ride the transport as far as they could, but thick vegetation obstructed their path, so they had to traverse the rest by foot. The taskforce came into conflict with Cartel forces along the way, although they failed to avert the Consortium's advance. Leaving the troops behind, Zann and Urai utilized their [[Stealth field generator/Legends|personal cloaking devices]] to bypass the [[Bp.2 anti-infantry turret|anti-infantry turrets]] and reach where the artifact was being held. Urai uncloaked and killed the Hutt mercenaries guarding the artifact, and Zann ordered him to take the relic and meet him at the rendezvous point. Just after the cloaked Urai left, Tyber was apprehended by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]], which Zann defeated, though many more Imperial forces arrived immediately afterwards. Outnumbered, he let them take him into custody, but vowed that they wouldn't hold him for long.<ref name="FOC" />

====Imprisoned====
[[File:Zann_Cot.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Tyber Zann sitting on his cot in the [[Kessel prison]].]]
While Zann rotted away in the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|spice mines]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], Urai Fen waited for the time Zann had set for his escape. By starting a [[Ground Battle on Kessel|riot]] on Kessel and hiring the crew of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to pick him up, Zann made his escape, narrowly avoiding execution from a [[Unidentified Mustafarian bounty hunter|bounty hunter]] hired by Jabba.

After his return to freedom, Zann, along with his closest companion Urai Fen, gradually reestablished the Consortium as a power to be reckoned with from their headquarters on [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]]. Among other things, the criminal organization saw a window of opportunity: the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] would distract both the Galactic Empire and the Rebel Alliance from the Zann Consortium's entreaties on planets. Having built up a sizable force of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], Tyber staged kidnappings, [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] raids and racketeering missions to coerce worlds into accepting his protection and allowing him to use their lanes to move his military. By these means of intimidation, the Zann Consortium was able to establish its influence on a number of big and small businesses such as the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]], the [[Plasma Energy Trade Commission]] and the [[Silica Tab]].

[[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Izbela Saarrj]] of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] wrote a report on the crime lord Zann's activities around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], mentioning his use of [[Spy/Legends|spies]] to study the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']], the first [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-class dreadnought]], while it underwent construction.<ref name="Scum and Villany">{{InsiderCite|89|Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy}}</ref> As [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''Eclipse'' was armed with an axial-super laser and was larger than any [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] created prior. Zann's interest was born out of a belief that the vessel's console would allow him to gain access to the wealth stored in Palpatine's fabled vaults, but he refrained from openly attacking the construction site above [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] to avoid the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet's]] wrath.

===Corrupting a galaxy===
[[File:ZannHolocronDathomir-FOC.jpg|thumb|left|Zann showing the stolen [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] to a [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsister]] on [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]].]]
Irritated by Jabba's repeated interference in his affairs, Zann organized an [[Battle of Saleucami (Zann Consortium)|attack]] on his competitor's holdings on [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]], wrestling control of a listening post. Learning of the existence of an old [[Geonosian/Legends|Geonosian]] [[Hypori droid factory|droid factory]] built on [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Zann Consortium moved to claim the foundry's [[Droideka Mark II]]s in order to better contend with the Hutt Cartel. Though it was revealed that Zann had been lured into a trap, he managed to reprogram the [[Droideka/Legends|Droidekas]] with Urai's help and overpower Jabba's lackeys.

In the battle's aftermath, Zann contacted Jabba and imposed a treaty on him. The Human crime lord stated that, in exchange for a ceasefire, he would not intrude on the Hutt Cartel's territories. In addition, Tyber demanded that he keep Saleucami and Hypori, which Jabba accepted.

Zann used his extensive criminal network to influence both the Empire and the Rebellion throughout the Galactic Civil War, siphoning their [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and sabotaging their operations when necessary. During the course of his ascent to power, his forces fought against both sides of the war. Zann had his own [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], an [[Aggressor-class destroyer|''Aggressor''-class destroyer]] named [[Merciless (Aggressor-class)|''Merciless'']], for his navy. When fighting, he would sometimes make use of his own [[Stealth field generator/Legends|personal cloaking device]] that rendered him invisible.

Zann was also intent on discovering the mysteries of the artefact he had stolen from Jabba back on Felucia. Informed by Urai of its nature as a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]], Tyber sought a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-wielder]] strong in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] to unlock its secrets. Knowing that he would find no help in this quest from either of the reigning [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]], he resolved to gain the assistance of the witches from [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] at Fenn's suggestion. Though the planet was under an Imperial blockade per the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] personal command, the crime lord and his [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] infiltrated the dark world. After freeing some of the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] and helping them gain vengeance on the local [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Dathomir)|Imperial Governor]], Zann persuaded [[Silri]], the most powerful of their number to decipher the [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifact]]. Though she knew of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] creation's true importance, she withheld information from Tyber causing them to quarrel verbally on several occasions. Shortly following the [[Ground Battle on Dathomir|ground battle on Dathomir]], the crime lord Tyber was contacted by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], who warned him that his rash behavior and unwise actions would soon earn him reprisals from Imperial forces.

[[File:Zann_screen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tyber Zann viewing the value and contents of Palpatine's vaults on board the [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']].]]
After the Empire seized [[Occupation of Bespin|control]] of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] contacted the crime lord Zann and offered him a job to steal [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna]] Gas from the [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] refinery which they would split in half. In exchange, the Black Sun Underlord would arrange a meeting with the Emperor or one of his [[Envoy/Legends|representatives]] to whom the Zann Consortium could sell the Sith artifact. Tyber and Urai knew this was a trap, but sought to use it to their advantage. Aware that the Imperial Military's [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]],<ref name="kenner">{{KennerCite|set=Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (toy line)|pack=Darth Vader vs. Prince Xizor|link=soteKxizor-vader.asp}}</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] himself, was on Bespin, they resolved to hire the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88]] to plant evidence that would implicate Prince Xizor and the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] enterprise. Tyber, Urai and their allies landed in Cloud City and sneaked their way through the Imperial garrison and out with their Tibanna cargo, barely escaping [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader's grip. As the Consortium forces escaped, a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] reported to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], notifying him that they had found evidence suggesting the [[Dark Prince]] of the Black Sun was the perpetrator behind the theft. With Xizor's assumed involvement in the Bespin robbery, Vader would later use this as an additional justification to execute him.<ref name="SotE novel">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref>

With the theft of the tibanna gas from Bespin complete, Xizor ostensibly held his end of the bargain and provided Tyber with the rendezvous coordinates for the meeting with Palpatine's representative. The negotiation proved to be a trap, with an Imperial fleet lying in wait above [[Carida/Legends|Carida]]. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Thrawn contacted the crime lord Zann, revealing that the false evidence had been enough for [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader to kill Prince Xizor, but had not been enough to disguise the Zann Consortium's unique signature. The [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] then engaged the gangster. In response, Tyber unveiled his new flagship, the [[Merciless (Aggressor-class)|''Merciless'']], and tested its destructive firepower on one of the Imperial vessels. During the battle, the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] bounty hunter [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]] stole the Sith artifact and gave it to Admiral Thrawn, who then fled in his flagship, the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial'' I-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Admonitor]]'', leaving the rest of his ships to their fates. After defeating the remainder of the Imperial fleet, Tyber returned to Ryloth.

After he and Silri had a "misunderstanding" over the information concealed within the Sith artifact, Tyber promised to have every Nightsister on Dathomir annihilated if she lied or withheld information again. He also made clear that his associate and friend, Urai Fen, would kill Silri if any attempt was made on Zann's life (as he mentioned to Tyber quite frequently).

Soon enough, Zann learned from his spies within the Rebellion that both Palpatine and Vader had left the capital world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for the forest moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. The crime lord saw the absence of both the Emperor and the Supreme Commander<ref name="kenner" /> as an opportunity for him to raid the archive on Imperial Center, where he had traced the lost artifact's signal. Tyber stole Palpatine's passkey to get on the ''Eclipse'' and Silri reclaimed the Sith holocron.

===Battle for the Eclipse===
{{Quote|Do you see Urai? Billions of credits! Technical data hoarded for decades! Priceless works of art! It's incredible! There is more wealth in these hidden vaults than even I can imagine!|Tyber Zann|<ref name="FOC" />}}
With the deaths of both Palpatine and Vader above Endor in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> Tyber seized his chance to move to Kuat and take over the ''Eclipse'', eager to gain intel on the Emperor's vaults. During the battle, the Zann Consortium briefly accepted an uneasy truce with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] Rebel forces to combat the local Imperial fleet, all while heading for the Star Dreadnaught. After infiltrating the ''Eclipse'' and hacking its console'','' Zann proceeded to steal tens of billions of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], technical data, and many priceless works of art from Palpatine's personal vault network, thus ensuring that he has enough resources to spread the Consortium throughout the galaxy. After his theft, Tyber decided to abandon the ''Eclipse'' as it would be a "target even the Rebels could not miss." He also decided to allow the Rebel Alliance to form their [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], expressing interest at the chance to "own a senator." The ''Eclipse'' was later recovered by Imperial forces, who continued its construction.

However, Silri secretly unlocked the Sith holocron behind Zann's and Fen's backs. The artifact led the Nightsister to an ancient Sith tomb, with thousands of [[Carbonite Sith Army|Sith soldiers]] frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]].

===Legacy===
Inspired by Zann's earlier riot, the prisoners in the spice mines of Kessel staged yet another revolt.<ref name="Alien Henchmen">{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-1|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I am in complete control, that's the way I like it.|Tyber Zann|<ref name="FOC" />}}
Zann was cold, methodical, ambitious, and ruthless, and had an arrogant streak, as evidenced by his steadfast confidence in himself. He liked to be in complete control of things and was also quite cunning. He was a criminal mastermind, a tactical genius, and adept at using most weapons of war. He could hold a grudge on people and would not let it go until he made them pay. Zann also seemed quite confident in his abilities, making his leadership abilities very powerful.<ref name="FOC" /> Zann's cruelty was expressed during the Felucia theft, when his scouts in Alpha Squad were killed by native spores, he expressed no sense of dismay or sadness, but said the ship would be a little bit lighter when they left the planet.

Despite these negatives traits, Tyber had a sense of humor, albeit a rather rude one, as seen throughout the Campaign towards Urai, such as when he asked Urai whether he had become a Jedi Knight during his absence due the reveal of the latter's Force connection. He was a long time ally of Urai Fen, who was possibly his only true friend, and the two were known to call each other by their first names. However, the crime lord cared very little for those who served him as soldiers and would kill those who disobeyed him even if their actions actually gained him something. Zann was also very tolerant of Silri's actions compared to Urai, who often wanted to kill her. The crime lord kept her around and he even claimed she had managed to grow on him, though he loathed to admit it.

Zann wielded a modified [[Rawk chopped special]], a [[double-barreled blaster carbine]] which was later favored by [[Cade Skywalker]] around [[130 ABY]].<ref name="Underworld News" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ZannConsortiumFelucia-FoC.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tyber Zann with his forces on [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]].]]

Zann was included in [[Star Wars Insider 89|''Star Wars Insider'' 89]]{{'s}} "[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]" at the request of [[LucasArts]], who wished additional publicity for him. For his illustration in ''Insider 89'', artist [[Joe Corroney]] based him on British actor [[Jason Isaacs]], using a promotional image of Isaacs' character [[W:c:harrypotter:Lucius Malfoy|Lucius Malfoy]] from the ''Harry Potter'' films as a reference.<ref>{{Facebook|username=joecorroney|url=joecorroney/posts/10152164796846879|name=[[Joe Corroney]]|quote=He was requested to be in the image by LucasArts because of the timing of the release of the game... Isaacs wasn't specifically requested for the model reference for the character, I made that call. But the character himself was requested to be shown in the artwork... I needed a more realistic looking model reference for a jumping off point to fit my realistic style of the other characters. Tyber Zann looked like Malfoy to me so I just found some photo reference of that character that worked for my artwork with his face in the angle I needed at the time.|archiveurl=https://archive.is/pA6n4}}</ref>

Zann was voiced by [[Steve Blum]] in the [[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption|''Forces of Corruption'']] trailer, and by [[Jake Eberle]] in the actual game.

This character name is often misspelled 'Zahn' as the gold pack version of the game's case featured this spelling. This error was corrected in later printings.
